steps.title: Identify the pronoun's antecedent
steps.content: The blank pronoun describes something that “debuted as part of the exhibit Women of Clay“; the main clause reveals “the artworks” as what debuted. Artworks is a plural noun.

steps.title: Apply pronoun-antecedent agreement
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: pronoun-antecedent agreement. Rule: a pronoun must match its antecedent in number. The antecedent is "the artworks" (plural).
  • Choice A — "they": plural pronoun matches the plural antecedent "artworks." ✓
  • Choice B — "that": singular relative pronoun; does not agree with the plural "artworks." ✗
  • Choice C — "it": singular pronoun; cannot refer to the plural "artworks." ✗
  • Choice D — "this": singular demonstrative pronoun; does not agree with the plural "artworks." ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
